overview: Sphere Shops is a Vintage control archetype that leverages artifact disruption and damage mitigation to outlast opponents. By deploying Null Rod and Sphere of Resistance, it severely hampers artifact-heavy strategies while stabilizing the board. Patchwork Automaton provides a resilient, low-cost threat that synergizes with the deck's defensive posture.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through resource denial and damage reduction
Deploying Patchwork Automaton as a resilient finisher
Capitalizing on opponent's inability to overcome Sphere of Resistance's cost increase

keep_criteria: Hands with early artifact disruption or interaction
Hands containing Sphere of Resistance or Null Rod
Hands with Patchwork Automaton and removal
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early answers to aggressive threats
Hands missing land or artifact synergy

early_game: Play lands and deploy Null Rod to disrupt artifact-dependent opponents
Hold Sphere of Resistance until you can protect it or use it to tax spells
mid_game: Stabilize with Sphere of Resistance to increase opponent's spell costs
Deploy Patchwork Automaton to apply pressure while maintaining defense
Use removal to clear opposing threats
late_game: Leverage Sphere of Resistance to shut down combo or aggressive strategies
Use Patchwork Automaton's toughness to outvalue opponents
Transition to a grindy endgame where resource denial wins

key_cards.name: Null Rod
key_cards.role: Disrupts artifact synergies and provides early board presence
key_cards.notes: Play early to counter artifact-heavy decks; remove or exile if opponent has non-artifact win conditions

key_cards.name: Sphere of Resistance
key_cards.role: Increases spell costs and reduces damage taken
key_cards.notes: Hold until you can answer removal or deploy it when opponent is casting high-cost spells; synergizes with control game plan

key_cards.name: Patchwork Automaton
key_cards.notes: Deploy after stabilizing; use its toughness to survive removal and close out games

sequencing_tips: Hold Sphere of Resistance until you can protect it or force opponent to waste removal
Play Null Rod on turn one against artifact decks, but be ready to discard it if it becomes a liability
Deploy Patchwork Automaton after clearing the board to maximize its impact
Prioritize land drops to ensure you can cast Sphere of Resistance when needed

matchups.advice: Lead with Null Rod to disrupt their engine; hold Sphere of Resistance to tax their win condition
Use Sphere of Resistance to reduce damage and tax removal; deploy Patchwork Automaton to stabilize
Race with Patchwork Automaton while using Sphere of Resistance to out-grind their resources

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Sphere Shops, Null Rod, Sphere of Resistance, Patchwork Automaton, Vintage

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Null Rod, Sphere of Resistance, Patchwork Automaton
